prevalence.trend: stable to increasing in some settings; polysubstance use and opioids remain concerns

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 criteria; assessment includes intoxication/withdrawal and comorbidity

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: moderate; many systems integrate screening in primary care and emergency settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improved with harm reduction and integrated treatment programs

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Access to addiction medicine and mental health services varies by member state
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Harm reduction (e.g., OST), psychosocial interventions, and integrated dual-diagnosis care where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overdose prevention, service gaps in marginalized populations, and variability across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established harm reduction infrastructure in many countries and strong public health monitoring (EMCDDA)
